lesson.subtitle: Aula 7 -- Procurement & Tenders -- Winning the Bid
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Natalie Viegas | Aula 7 -- Procurement & Tenders

listenings.text: Hello Natalie, this is Mr. Silva from the Ministry of Justice procurement office. I am calling about the cloud services tender. The deadline for bids is next Friday at five. If your team submits the bid on time, we will start the technical review on Monday. Please remember the compliance documents. If a vendor forgets the compliance forms, the bid is not valid. We will shortlist three vendors, and then we will negotiate the final price. One more thing about the contract: there is a new clause about data security. If you accept this clause, we will move to the next stage. Send me your quotation and your approval letter before the deadline. Thank you, and have a good day.

Hi team, this is Natalie with a quick update on the Ministry tender. Here are our next steps. First, I will send the quotation today. If finance approves it this afternoon, we will be inside the deadline. Second, please check the compliance documents. If one form is missing, the whole bid is not valid. Third, there is a new clause about data security. If our legal team accepts the clause, we will sign on Monday. If the client shortlists us, we will negotiate the final price next week. Could you send me the technical part by tomorrow morning? Thank you, everyone. This is a big opportunity for our public sector pipeline.

extra_audio.text: First, the client opens a tender for the new project. Then several vendors prepare their bids and send a quotation. After that, the client checks compliance and shortlists the best vendors. Next, the two sides negotiate the price and the main clauses. Finally, the client gives approval and both sides sign the contract.
